01_initial_request.input_data: N9 microglial cells, SN56 neuronal cells, thiamine-deficient medium (0.003 mmol/L thiamine), control medium (0.009 mmol/L thiamine), Zinc (0.10 mmol/L), Amprolium
01_initial_request.user_intent: To investigate the alterations of energy metabolism enzymes in microglia in the presence of thiamine deficiency and Zn excess and determine how these signals affect the interaction of microglia with cholinergic neuronal cells
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Cell viability, acetyl-CoA levels, intracellular free Zn accumulation, and activities of energy metabolism enzymes (PDHC, TCA cycle)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] N9 microglial cells and SN56 cholinergic neuronal cells need to be prepared for experimentation. [Gap] Cells need to be differentiated to achieve cholinergic phenotype for SN56. [Decision] To achieve proper cell preparation, chose to use cell culture method to perform differentiation on N9 and SN56 cells, expecting to obtain differentiated cells ready for treatment.

parameters.cell_lines: N9 microglial cell line (RRID: CVCL 0452) and SN56.B5G4 cholinergic neuroblastoma cells (RRID: CVCL 4456)
parameters.differentiation_factors: 1 mmol/L dibutyryl cAMP and 0.001 mmol/L all-trans-retinoic acid
parameters.medium: Minimal Eagle Medium (MEM) with 1 mmol/L L-glutamine, 0.05 mg streptomycin, 50 U penicillin per 1 mL, 10% fetal bovine serum

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Cells were differentiated and verified for phenotype. SN56 cells verified by acetylcholine level measurement (no less than 30 pmols/mg of protein). N9 cells verified by IL-6 release aggravation by LPS.

references.relevance: Supports differentiation protocol for cholinergic phenotype

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Cells are prepared and need thiamine-deficient conditions. [Gap] Need to establish thiamine deficiency vs control conditions. [Decision] To achieve thiamine deficiency modeling, chose to use medium preparation method to perform thiamine concentration adjustment on culture medium, expecting to obtain TD medium (0.003 mmol/L) and control medium (0.009 mmol/L).
02_agent_trajectory.observation: TD medium caused significant decreases in ThDP in both cell groups. Control ThDP levels in N9 cells: 189.2 pmol/mg protein (control) vs 150 pmol/mg protein (TD). SN56 cells: 92 pmol/mg protein (control) vs 70 pmol/mg protein (TD).

references.relevance: Supports thiamine concentration standards for cell culture

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Thiamine deficiency established but needs aggravation. [Gap] Need to further reduce intracellular ThDP levels. [Decision] To achieve aggravated ThDP deficiency, chose to use amprolium treatment to perform thiamine transport inhibition on cells, expecting to obtain concentration-dependent ThDP depletion.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 80% decrease in intracellular ThDP content in N9 caused by 10 mmol/L amprolium. IC50 equal to 2.34 mmol/L for ThDP level in N9 cells. In SN56, 5 mmol/L amprolium caused 20% decrease in ThDP but 35% decrease in cell count.

references.relevance: Supports amprolium as competitive inhibitor of thiamine transport

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] ThDP deficiency established. [Gap] Need to introduce Zn excess condition. [Decision] To achieve Zn excess modeling, chose to use zinc treatment to perform Zn exposure on cells, expecting to obtain Zn accumulation and toxicity effects.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: N9 microglial cells displayed no susceptibility to 0.10 mmol/L Zn concentrations. SN56 cells showed 56% inhibition of growth rate and 53% inhibition of viability. Zn inhibited SN56 PDHC, aconitase and ICDH-NADP activities by 56%, 67% and 85% respectively.

references.relevance: Supports Zn concentration effects on neuronal cells

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Individual cell treatments completed. [Gap] Need to test microglia-neuron interactions. [Decision] To achieve co-culture interaction testing, chose to use insert co-culture method to perform N9-SN56 co-culturing, expecting to obtain data on microglial protective effects.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Co-culture of SN56 with N9 cells reduced thiamine-deficiency-Zn-evoked inhibition of acetyl-CoA metabolism and restored viability. N9 inserts decreased basal Zn level in SN56 by 45%.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Treatments completed. [Gap] Need to assess cell viability. [Decision] To achieve viability assessment, chose to use trypan blue exclusion assay to perform cell viability measurement on treated cells, expecting to obtain viable cell counts and death fractions.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: N9 cell viability limited by 5% with 10 mmol/L amprolium. SN56 showed 12% increase in TB-positive cells with 5 mmol/L amprolium, increasing to 25% at 10 mmol/L. Zn+amprolium caused 60% loss of viability in SN56, reduced to 20% with N9 inserts.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Viability assessed. [Gap] Need to measure intracellular ThDP levels. [Decision] To achieve ThDP quantification, chose to use HPLC with electrochemical detector to perform ThDP measurement on cell extracts, expecting to obtain ThDP concentration data.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: ThDP levels: N9 control 189.2 pmol/mg protein, TD 150 pmol/mg. SN56 control 92 pmol/mg, TD 70 pmol/mg. 80% decrease with 10 mmol/L amprolium in N9 TD medium.

references.relevance: Supports HPLC method for thiamine quantification

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] ThDP levels measured. [Gap] Need to assess PDHC enzyme activity. [Decision] To achieve PDHC activity measurement, chose to use citrate synthase coupled method to perform PDHC assay on cell samples, expecting to obtain enzyme activity data.

parameters.cofactors: 2 mmol/L MgCl₂, 10 mmol/L dithiotreitol, 2 mmol/L NAD, 0.20 mmol/L CoA, 2.5 mmol/L oxaloacetic acid, 0.15 IU citrate synthase, 2 mmol/L thiamine pyrophosphate

02_agent_trajectory.observation: PDHC activity inhibited by 65% with 10 mmol/L amprolium in N9. IC50 for amprolium against PDHC: 3.8 mmol/L in N9, 17.5 mmol/L in SN56. Complete (100%) inhibition in SN56 with Zn+amprolium combination.

references.relevance: Supports citrate synthase coupled method for PDHC

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] PDHC activity measured. [Gap] Need to assess aconitase enzyme activity. [Decision] To achieve aconitase activity measurement, chose to use NADP reduction method to perform aconitase assay on cell samples, expecting to obtain enzyme activity data.

parameters.cofactors: 2 mmol/L MgCl₂, 0.10 mmol/L NADP, 1 IU isocitrate dehydrogenase

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Aconitase activity inhibited by 40% with 10 mmol/L amprolium in N9. IC50 equal to 12.5 mmol/L. In SN56, Zn inhibited aconitase by 67%.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Aconitase activity measured. [Gap] Need to assess ICDH-NADP enzyme activity. [Decision] To achieve ICDH-NADP activity measurement, chose to use NADP reduction method to perform ICDH-NADP assay on cell samples, expecting to obtain enzyme activity data.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: ICDH-NADP activities in N9 were not affected by intracellular ThDP shortages. In SN56, Zn inhibited ICDH-NADP by 85%. ICDH-NADP in SN56 was 90% inhibited with Zn+amprolium, whereas in N9 it remained resistant.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Enzyme activities measured. [Gap] Need to measure acetyl-CoA levels. [Decision] To achieve acetyl-CoA quantification, chose to use cycling method to perform acetyl-CoA measurement on cell extracts, expecting to obtain acetyl-CoA concentration data.

parameters.CoA-SH_removal: maleic anhydride in ethyl ether for 2 h

02_agent_trajectory.observation: 10 mmol/L amprolium decreased acetyl-CoA levels in N9 by 30% with IC50 18.8 mmol/L. SN56 neurons showed 16 times more sensitivity to ThDP deficits than microglial cells. N9 inserts partially restored acetyl-CoA levels in SN56.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Acetyl-CoA levels measured. [Gap] Need to measure intracellular Zn accumulation. [Decision] To achieve Zn quantification, chose to use fluorimetric TSQ method to perform intracellular Zn measurement on cell extracts, expecting to obtain Zn concentration data.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: N9 inserts decreased basal Zn level in SN56 by 45%, below control value. Zn accumulation does not depend on ThDP status in both cell types. 146% increase in Zn accumulation in N9 with thiamine-supplemented medium despite no viability changes.

references.relevance: Supports TSQ fluorimetric method for Zn measurement

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Zn levels measured. [Gap] Need to assess cytokine release for microglial activation. [Decision] To achieve cytokine quantification, chose to use ELISA to perform IL-6 measurement on culture medium, expecting to obtain IL-6 concentration data.

parameters.kit: Platinum ELISA kits (eBioscience, Bender MedSystems GmbH)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: IL-6 concentrations remained at similar levels irrespective of experimental conditions. N9 inserts did not significantly increase IL-6 release. Bulk of IL-6 originated from neuronal cells.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] IL-6 measured. [Gap] Need to assess TNF-α release for microglial activation. [Decision] To achieve cytokine quantification, chose to use ELISA to perform TNF-α measurement on culture medium, expecting to obtain TNF-α concentration data.

parameters.kit: Platinum ELISA kits (eBioscience, Bender MedSystems GmbH)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: N9 inserts resulted in 10-fold increase in accumulated TNF-α in thiamine-deficient medium. TNF-α reduced by 70% upon addition of amprolium with Zn. 90% of TNF-α originated from microglial cells.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] All experimental measurements completed. [Gap] Need to validate microglial functional capacity. [Decision] To achieve functional validation, chose to use LPS stimulation to perform microglial activation test on N9 cells, expecting to confirm microglial responsiveness.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: LPS resulted in 70-fold increase in IL-6 and 825-fold increase in TNF-α levels in N9 culture medium, confirming microglial functional capacity.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Multi-parametric cellular metabolism and viability assessment including enzyme activity assays, metabolite quantification, and cell viability measurements

cell_viability_N9.interpretation: N9 cells showed only 5% viability loss with 10 mmol/L amprolium in TD medium, indicating resistance to ThDP depletion

cell_viability_SN56.interpretation: SN56 cells showed 25-60% viability loss with amprolium+Zn, reduced to 20% loss with N9 co-culture

ThDP_decrease_N9.interpretation: Maximum ThDP decrease in N9 with 10 mmol/L amprolium in TD medium

PDHC_inhibition_SN56.interpretation: Complete PDHC inhibition in SN56 with Zn+amprolium combination

acetyl-CoA_decrease_N9.interpretation: Acetyl-CoA decrease in N9 with 10 mmol/L amprolium, IC50 18.8 mmol/L

Zn_accumulation_reduction.interpretation: N9 inserts decreased basal Zn level in SN56 by 45%, demonstrating protective Zn uptake

neuronal_sensitivity_ratio.interpretation: Cholinergic neuronal cells were 16 times more sensitive to intracellular ThDP deficits than microglial cells based on acetyl-CoA vs PDHC correlation slopes

03_success_verification.final_verdict: Research objective achieved. The study successfully demonstrated differential sensitivity of N9 microglial and SN56 neuronal cells to thiamine deficiency combined with Zn excess. N9 microglia showed resistance to TD-Zn toxicity while SN56 neurons were highly susceptible. Co-culture with N9 cells provided partial neuroprotection by reducing intracellular Zn accumulation in neurons and restoring acetyl-CoA metabolism. Key findings include: (1) N9 cells maintain energy metabolism enzyme activities under TD-Zn conditions while SN56 shows severe PDHC inhibition; (2) Microglial cells can uptake excess Zn from neuronal environment; (3) ThDP supplementation can increase brain cell resistance to Zn excess. These findings support the hypothesis that microglia may display neuroprotective properties under specific neurodegenerative conditions.
